# Load the quranic_verses.json file to search for the given phrase.
import json, os, sys, re
path = "/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    q = json.load(f)
len(q), list(q.keys())[:5] if isinstance(q, dict) else None

# inspect first entry
q[0]

# Load quranic verses and build normalized text for each ayah.
import re, unicodedata
def normalize_ar(text):
    # remove tashkeel
    tashkeel = re.compile('[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]')
    t = tashkeel.sub('', text)
    # remove tatweel
    t = t.replace('ـ','')
    # unify alef variants
    t = t.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا')
    # unify hamza on waw/yaa to simple forms? replace 'ؤ'->'و' and 'ئ'->'ي' maybe
    t = t.replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي')
    # replace alef maqsura with ya
    t = t.replace('ى','ي')
    # replace taa marbuta with ه?? keep as is; often best to replace with ه to match
    # but many DB keep ة; let's replace to ه? Risky. We'll keep it
    # replace different kaph forms (Persian ک) to Arabic ك
    t = t.replace('ک','ك')
    # remove punctuation and symbols
    t = re.sub(r'[^\w\s\u0600-\u06FF]', ' ', t) # keep Arabic letters and underscore maybe; convert others to space
    # collapse spaces
    t = re.sub(r'\s+',' ', t).strip()
    return t

norms = []
for item in q:
    ay = item['ayah_text']
    norms.append(normalize_ar(ay))
len(norms)

full_span = "وَلَقَدْ أَرَکنا فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَخْذولًا فَأَخَذَ عَلَيْهِ عَقدًا أَن لَا يُؤْتِيَ إِلَى اللَّهِ عَقدًا"
snippet = "وَلَقَدْ أَرَکنا فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَخْذولًا فَأَخَذَ عَلَيْهِ عَ عقدًا".replace(" ","") # hmm snippet provided earlier
# actually the provided snippet in prompt was "وَلَقَدْ أَرَکنا فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَخْذولًا فَأَخَذَ عَلَيْهِ عَقدًا أَن لَا يُؤْتِيَ إِلَى اللَّهِ عَقدًا"
# But there was shorter "وَلَقَدْ أَرَکنا ف...َهِ عَقدًا" But we'll use provided full span and partial snippet as they gave.
norm_full = normalize_ar(full_span)
norm_snip = normalize_ar("وَلَقَدْ أَرَکنا فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَخْذولًا فَأَخَذَ عَلَيْهِ عَ عقدًا")
norm_full, norm_snip

cands = [(i, item) for i,item in enumerate(q) if 'فرعون' in normalize_ar(item['ayah_text'])]
len(cands), [item['ayah_text'] for i,item in cands[:10]]

surah_40 = [item for item in q if item['surah_id']==40]
len(surah_40), surah_40[:5]

# compute token-overlap F1 similarity
def token_f1(a,b):
    ta = a.split()
    tb = b.split()
    set_a = ta  # treat as list for counts? But we can count multisets
    from collections import Counter
    ca = Counter(ta)
    cb = Counter(tb)
    overlap = 0
    for k in ca:
        overlap += min(ca[k], cb.get(k,0))
    if len(ta)+len(tb)==0:
        return 1.0
    f1 = 2*overlap/(len(ta)+len(tb))
    return f1

norm_full = normalize_ar(full_span)
best = []
for i,item in enumerate(q):
    n = normalize_ar(item['ayah_text'])
    sim = token_f1(norm_full, n)
    if sim > 0:
        best.append((sim, item['surah_id'], item['ayah_id'], item['ayah_text'], n))
# sort by sim desc
best_sorted = sorted(best, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_sorted[:20]
[x for x in q if 'مَخْذُول' in x['ayah_text']][:10]
[x for x in q if 'عقد' in x['ayah_text']][:50]
[x for x in q if 'عقد' in normalize_ar(x['ayah_text'])][:50]

# Get ghafir 40:36 from DB
ghafir_36 = next(item for item in q if item['surah_id']==40 and item['ayah_id']==36)
ghafir_36_text = ghafir_36['ayah_text']
print("Ghafir 40:36:", ghafir_36_text)
print("Normalized:", normalize_ar(ghafir_36_text))

# compute token_f1 with norm_full
n1 = normalize_ar(full_span)
n2 = normalize_ar(ghafir_36_text)
print("token_f1:", token_f1(n1, n2))

# compute LCS length ratio (longest common substring)
def lcs_length(a,b):
    # compute longest common substring length for strings
    la, lb = len(a), len(b)
    dp = [[0]*(lb+1) for _ in range(la+1)]
    best = 0
    for i in range(la):
        for j in range(lb):
            if a[i]==b[j]:
                dp[i+1][j+1] = dp[i][j] + 1
                if dp[i+1][j+1] > best:
                    best = dp[i+1][j+1]
    return best

lcs = lcs_length(n1, n2)
ratio = lcs / max(len(n1), len(n2))
lcs, ratio

import difflib, math, heapq
def seq_ratio(a,b):
    return difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, a, b).ratio()

norm_target = normalize_ar(full_span)
best_seq = []
for item in q:
    n = normalize_ar(item['ayah_text'])
    r = seq_ratio(norm_target, n)
    best_seq.append((r, item['surah_id'], item['ayah_id'], item['ayah_text'], n))
best_seq_sorted = sorted(best_seq, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_seq_sorted[:20]

# Load hadith DB
hpath = "/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json"
with open(hpath, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    had = json.load(f)
len(had)
had[0]

# Search hadiths for tokens
tokens = ['فرعون','مخذول','مخذولًا','أخذ','فأخذ','عقد','عهد','لا يؤتى','يؤتى']
matches_h = []
for i,item in enumerate(had):
    txts = []
    if item.get('hadithTxt'):
        txts.append(item['hadithTxt'])
    if item.get('Matn'):
        txts.append(item['Matn'])
    combined = " ".join([t for t in txts if t])
    nn = normalize_ar(combined)
    if any(t in nn for t in ['فرعون','مخذول','فاخذ','عقد','عهد','لا يوتي','يوتي الى الله','يوتى']):
        matches_h.append((i, item['hadithID'], item['title'], nn[:200]))
len(matches_h), matches_h[:20]

# Compute best sequence matching ratio across hadith texts
import difflib, tqdm
best_h = []
norm_target = normalize_ar(full_span)
for i,item in enumerate(had):
    txts = []
    if item.get('hadithTxt'):
        txts.append(item['hadithTxt'])
    if item.get('Matn'):
        txts.append(item['Matn'])
    combined = " ".join([t for t in txts if t])
    n = normalize_ar(combined)
    r = difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, norm_target, n).ratio()
    best_h.append((r, i, item.get('hadithID'), item.get('title')))
# sort top 20
best_h_sorted = sorted(best_h, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_h_sorted[:20]

# search for verses that mention فرعون and have 'ار' near by
candidates = []
for item in q:
    txt = item['ayah_text']
    n = normalize_ar(txt)
    if 'فرعون' in n and ('ار' in n or 'رينا' in n or 'ارينا' in n):
        candidates.append((item['surah_id'], item['ayah_id'], txt, n))
candidates[:50], len(candidates)
